achievements.description: GDCU-8893 Gambling.com Design System (Ciara Carroll, Highest) → Done ✅ at 12:24 BST. Sprint's highest-priority ticket signed off and closed. Deliverables: refined design system tokens, Figma DS Token Exporter plugin, Claude knowledge files (.md), AI Tool Usage Guidelines, updated Figma design system. Companion duplicate ticket GDCU-8944 also cancelled. Stakeholders: Colin Brannigan, Chloe Christie, Gab Cornoiu, Eoin McCarthy.

GDCU-8813 Ace Navigation Assistant UI Audit (Miguel Migneco) → Done ✅ at 09:51 BST. Audit of Ace nav assistant UI for redundancy after global nav bar integration completed and signed off. Figma prototype with annotations delivered; dev handoff includes clear list of components to remove/modify.
GDCU-8866 Automation Bug Fixing & Refactoring Sprint 8 (Robin John) → Done ✅ at 09:39 BST. Sprint 8 automation suite bugs fixed and code refactored.

GDCU-8512 Always On Betting Tips Page (Miguel Migneco) → Done ✅ at 12:26 BST. 8-day stalled In Product review cleared. Mobile-first Betting Tips Hub with live tip cards, sport nav tabs, Matrix widget integration, and operator offers module signed off.

Sprint 169 / Delivery Cycle 8 Q2 Week 1 & 2 released successfully. Morning hold (called by Nebojsa Stanic at 08:29 BST) was resolved and publishes resumed across all geos (CA, UK, NZ, RO, DE, BE, ES). Chloe Christie posted release announcement in #gdc-core: Sign In/Registration Modal Redesign live across all geos; Bottom Navigation Bar Mobile A/B Test live on UK geo; Card Carousel Shortcode live.

Carlos Blauth hotfixed giveaways page image layout issue (gambling.com/uk/giveaways). Ciara Carroll flagged a broken 2-card layout in #gdc-pod; Carlos investigated and pushed a hotfix. Chloe Christie confirmed fix was acceptable. Ciara created GDCU-8947 (Cosmo Cash Machine Images) as a follow-up for further image/font updates.

GDCU-8935 World Cup Predictor Game (Miguel Migneco) added to Backlog at 11:08 BST. New flagship FTP game: full end-to-end Figma design for FIFA World Cup 2026 predictor — group stage + knockout + outrights, £10k prize pool, private leagues, IUJ chip integration (2 chips/match day). Mobile-first (320/768/1280px).

GDCU-8895 US Prediction Market Page Not on Sitemap (Sanja Budic, High) moved to In Product at 15:37 BST — ready for Mike's sign-off. Requirements: new Prediction header on US sitemap, compliance-safe placement (not under Betting header), logic for future brand reviews under a 'Prediction Reviews' heading.

Backlog hygiene: 6 tickets Cancelled today — GDCU-8916 (Add to Compare US Casino), GDCU-8917 (Add to Compare US Betting), GDCU-8918 (Add to Compare US DFS/Poker), GDCU-8737 (Slot Game Hub Filter placeholder), GDCU-8944 (duplicate Genesis Design System), GDCU-8901 (Instagram Oplist NZ operator images).
CRM Journey Tracker updated in Confluence (AdOps) — 25 journeys now live across 14 markets. UK Casino, UK Sport, IE Casino all have Welcome, Cross-sell, and Re-engagement flows active.

meeting_summaries.title: No meetings recorded for 01/05/2026
meeting_summaries.summary: No meeting notes found in Confluence for today. No meetings were listed in the morning report (Microsoft 365 calendar access unavailable). No meeting-related Slack threads identified across monitored channels today.

action_points.action: CRITICAL CARRY-OVER — GDCU-8619 Operator List Redesign (Ciara Carroll, Blocked). Day 15 blocked — last updated 30/04 14:37, no progress today. Three sprint iterations without resolution. Must be escalated to Colin Brannigan if not cleared first thing on next working day. Scope: 3 distinct grid-format operator list iterations for UK casino page, mobile-first.

NEW — Review and sign off GDCU-8895 US Prediction Market Page Not on Sitemap (Sanja Budic, High). Moved to In Product today at 15:37 BST. Requirements: add Prediction header to US sitemap; link to /us/prediction-markets; NOT under any Betting header (compliance requirement); future brand reviews to appear under a 'Prediction Reviews' heading. Stakeholders: Michael Butlin, Colin Brannigan, Eoin McCarthy, Joshua Smith.

CARRY-OVER — Review and sign off GDCU-8736 Learning Hub Imagery (Ciara Carroll, Medium). Still In Product (last updated 30/04 12:35). Sign-off unblocks Carlos Blauth's dev work on GDCU-8733 (Apply Feedback to Learning Hub — still In Progress). Stakeholders: Gab Cornoiu, Ciara, Mike, Carlos, Joshua Smith.

CARRY-OVER — GDCU-8792 Cloudflare Bot Management (Sanja Budic, Blocked in QA). Still blocked since 23/04 — Day 8 with no movement. Now that Sanja has progressed GDCU-8895 to In Product, follow up with her on what's specifically blocking GDCU-8792 in QA.

CARRY-OVER — GDCU-8842 Genesis Content Layouts (Harden Thomas Wallace) still In Progress (last updated today 16:23 BST). Related to Sprint 169 morning hold. Monitor whether Harden completes for this release or carries to next sprint. Stakeholders: Gab Cornoiu, Chloe Christie.

CARRY-OVER — GDCU-8834 Cool Tones Fixes (Harden Thomas Wallace) still In Progress after QA failure on 29/04. Multiple visual issues: CA/FR table contrast, toast message colour, copy/paste background, button styling, navigation boxes accessibility failure. Confirm with Harden whether this makes the current release or is deferred to next sprint.

CARRY-OVER — GDCU-8879 Genesis Brand Carousel Component Card (Jessica Dordevic) still In Progress (last updated today 15:28). Bounced from In Product — confirm with Jessica whether she is revising or waiting on Chloe's Origins logo feedback before re-submitting.

CARRY-OVER — GDCU-8546 Tinder Style Offer Match (Jessica Dordevic) still In Progress (last updated today 15:27). Bounced from In Product — confirm with Jessica what revision is underway before it re-enters the review queue.

CARRY-OVER — GDCU-8928 IUJ Banners (Jessica Dordevic) still In Progress (last updated 30/04 12:51). Quick ticket created by Mike — two banners: (1) completing additional profile page earns chips; (2) recent iPhone winner banner for IUJ Rewards Page and Home Page. Confirm Jessica has what she needs.

NEW — GDCU-8947 Cosmo Cash Machine Images (Ciara Carroll) created and In Progress today. Cosmo Cash Machine giveaway on gambling.com/uk/giveaways needs: updated article image, hero image, font updates, coming-soon images, and email images (jackpot £5,000; wheel prizes: £1,000, £500, £250, 10x draw entries). Confirm with Ciara that Carlos hotfix was sufficient or if further image work is needed.

NEW — Design decision needed for GDCU-8787 Carousel Banner Shortcode. Joshua Smith raised in #gdc-product-design-pod: team must decide whether to (a) reuse the Simple Banner shortcode and add arrows, or (b) use the Free Games-style banner (cannot be reused elsewhere). Joshua noted 'one for when you're all back from bank holidays' — indicates this needs a response next working day.

NEW — Sprint planning: schedule GDCU-8935 World Cup Predictor Game (Miguel Migneco, Backlog). Comprehensive design ticket for flagship FTP World Cup 2026 game created today. Full Figma flow: entry → predictions (group + knockout + outrights) → leaderboard → results → re-engagement. All breakpoints. Discuss sprint slot with Miguel and Chloe.

CARRY-OVER — Admin Panel cache issue for Dean Ryan. Carlos Blauth flagged in #gdc-pod that Dean Ryan reported admin/geos not working — cache-related, Carlos investigating a broader fix. Monitor whether this is resolved or needs PM escalation.

CARRY-OVER — Vic Dadson expected back from sick leave on Tuesday 05/05/2026 (Monday 04/05 is UK May Bank Holiday). Prepare for her return: data engineering options discussion flagged in previous reports. Plan any outstanding analytics handover.

CARRY-OVER — Gabriel Cornoiu stakeholder intake questions still outstanding: (1) should there be an 'Other' option in the offer ordering dropdown? (2) does 'new ordering structure required' mean a new OpList? Last DM from Gab was 23/04.

CARRY-OVER — GDCU-8846 Cosmo Spins Spring Ad Campaign (Ciara Carroll, To Do). Creative direction from Mike needed. Confluence CRO meeting note for today: 'Cosmo Spins not live yet, waiting on...' — confirms live-blocked on Mike's direction. Josh Kerr feedback: clearer message, less gaming feel.

CARRY-OVER — GDCU-8903 Sports Quiz Hub UX pre-req ticket not yet created. Ticket states design handoff must be completed and approved before dev begins. Create the UX pre-req or validate scope with Chloe and Gab before next sprint planning.

calendar._note: Microsoft 365 authentication not completed — Outlook calendar unavailable. No agenda pages found in Confluence for 04/05/2026 or 05/05/2026. Monday 04/05/2026 is the UK Early May Bank Holiday — first working day back is likely Tuesday 05/05/2026, which is also the date Vic Dadson is expected back from sick leave.

next_working_day.title: UK Early May Bank Holiday — Monday 04/05/2026
next_working_day.synopsis: 04/05/2026 is the UK Early May Bank Holiday. If observed by GDC Group, the next working day is Tuesday 05/05/2026. Vic Dadson is expected back from sick leave on 05/05. Joshua Smith's #gdc-product-design-pod message today referenced 'when you're all back from bank holidays', confirming team absence expected Monday.
next_working_day.prep_notes: First day back (Tuesday 05/05 if bank holiday): (1) Escalate GDCU-8619 block to Colin Brannigan — now Day 15+, overdue. (2) Sign off GDCU-8895 Prediction Market Sitemap (High, In Product). (3) Respond to Joshua's #gdc-product-design-pod design decision on GDCU-8787 Carousel Banner Shortcode. (4) Welcome Vic Dadson back — schedule data engineering options discussion. (5) Sprint planning input: schedule GDCU-8935 World Cup Predictor Game.

in_product_eod.vs_morning: Reduced from 6 → 3. GDCU-8893, GDCU-8813, GDCU-8866, GDCU-8512 all moved to Done today.

_meta.sprint_release: Sprint 169 released successfully after morning hold. Delivery Cycle 8 Q2 features live: Sign-In Modal Redesign (all geos), Bottom Nav A/B Test (UK), Card Carousel Shortcode.
_meta.confluence_note: CQL with 'lastModified >= today' returned 400 — fixed with explicit timestamp. 20 recent pages returned, none in GDC space with today's meeting notes. Most active spaces today: GDC Core (SEO disavow), Technology (KTag docs, Infra report), AdOps (CRM tracker), CRO/Growth (Reece meeting), Bookies POD (DSU).
_meta.slack_note: Active channels today: #gdc-pod (giveaways hotfix, admin panel issue), #gdc-product-design-pod (Cosmo Cash Machine ticket, carousel banner shortcode decision), #gdc-pdmt (Gab available), #gdc-core (Delivery Cycle 8 release announcement). Silent channels: #gdc-product-led-growth, #core-product, #gdc-f2p-apps, #gdc-high-agency, #6amigos.
_meta.calendar_note: Microsoft 365 OAuth not completed. Monday 04/05/2026 = UK Early May Bank Holiday. No Confluence agenda pages for next week yet. Complete OAuth to enable calendar integration.
key_highlights: 4 tickets signed off and moved to Done — biggest single-day clearance this sprint
GDCU-8893 Gambling.com Design System (Highest priority) closed — major milestone for AI design tooling
Sprint 169 / Delivery Cycle 8 Q2 released successfully after morning hold
GDCU-8935 World Cup Predictor Game added to backlog — new major FTP initiative
GDCU-8619 Operator List Redesign: Day 15 Blocked — escalation overdue
Monday 04/05 = UK May Bank Holiday — next working day likely Tuesday 05/05 (also Vic's return)
